title: Indulgent Egyptian Aubergine Crumble
description: A nutty, crumbly vegan dish featuring baby aubergines made in a tandoor clay pot, inspired by Egyptian flavors.

ingredients.name: olive oil, infused with dried mint

instructions: Preheat your tandoor-clay-pot over medium heat until it starts to smoke lightly, then remove and wipe clean.
Slice 400g baby aubergines in half lengthwise and brush both sides with 20ml infused olive oil seasoned with dried mint.
Season the aubergines with 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground coriander, 1 tsp smoked paprika, and 1 tsp salt, then place them cut side down in the hot pot.
Roast the aubergines for 12 minutes, flipping once, until they are tender and lightly charred, then transfer them to a plate.

In a bowl, combine 60g all-purpose flour, 30g chopped almonds, 15g toasted sesame seeds, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground coriander, 1 tsp smoked paprika, and 1 tsp dried mint, stirring to even distribution.
Add 2 tbsp tahini, 1 tbsp lemon juice, and a pinch of salt to the dry mix, stirring until a crumbly paste forms.
Return the roasted aubergines to the pot, arrange them in a single layer, and spoon the crumble mixture evenly over the top.
Cover the pot with its lid and cook on low heat for 10 minutes, allowing the crumble to set and flavors to meld.
Remove the lid, broil the pot for 2 minutes to lightly brown the crumble topping, then turn off the heat.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving warm, garnished with fresh mint leaves if desired.
